canonical_title: Pesticide groundwater exposure & risk-assessment coursework (Exercises E_1/E_2/E_3)
description: A graded academic assignment in chemical risk assessment: computing dietary/water exposure, Hazard Quotients, ADI %coverage, Margins of Exposure, and a probabilistic (Beta-distribution) intake assessment across population groups, with a Matriculation Number and 'Expected answer' sections. The user drives the work over ~2 hours across three exercises.
why_it_matters: This is the user's real, owned, multi-step deliverable; storing it supports reconstructing their academic workload and toxicology coursework.
confidence_rationale: Strong explicit evidence: matriculation number, structured exercises, expected-answer sections, sustained user-directed work across E_1–E_3.
uncertainties: Course/institution name not stated in bundle
Whether this is graded vs. self-study (matriculation number implies graded)
contradictions: Selection labelled this conversation false_project, but evidence supports a real owned assignment
inclusion_rationale: Meets six strict-project criteria as an academic assignment; classified as assignment rather than project to respect the more specific type.

canonical_title: Workflow: user relays Claude-in-Excel actions, Claude-the-chat drafts the prompts
description: User establishes a repeated working pattern: the chat assistant writes prompts/instructions, the user pastes them into 'Claude in Excel', then pastes the Excel agent's results back for verification. This is a deliberately adopted, repeatedly used multi-tool workflow.
why_it_matters: A genuine adopted workflow (not a mere tool trace) that defines how the user gets the assignment done; useful for reconstructing how they work.
confidence_rationale: User explicitly sets up and repeatedly enforces the relay pattern ('you won't be driving you will be giving instructions to claude in excel', 'just give me the prompt').
inclusion_rationale: Qualifies as meaningful workflow knowledge because the user is adopting and repeatedly using the tool combination, not merely invoking a tool once.

canonical_title: Claude in Excel (tool the user is learning and adopting)
description: Claude's Excel integration, which the user is new to and is actively learning to use to complete the risk-assessment exercises.
why_it_matters: Context entity meaningful because the user is evaluating/adopting it, not merely a passive tool trace.
confidence_rationale: User explicitly states they are new to Claude in Excel and want to advance effectively with it.
inclusion_rationale: Adoption/evaluation by the user elevates this tool to a context entity.

canonical_title: Decision: assistant should generate prompts for the Excel agent rather than answer directly
description: User decides the chat assistant must not 'drive' directly but instead produce prompts for Claude-in-Excel; reinforced multiple times.
why_it_matters: A user-authored process decision that shapes the whole conversation's method.
confidence_rationale: Explicit, user-authored, repeated.

canonical_title: Constraint: advance step-by-step without skipping steps (user is a beginner)
description: User requires careful, incremental progress 'while not jumping steps' because they are new to the tool, and later asks the Excel agent to 'move incrementally'.
why_it_matters: A stable working preference/constraint that should inform how future help is given.
confidence_rationale: Explicit in this conversation; whether it generalizes beyond this assignment is uncertain.
uncertainties: Scope: assignment-specific vs. general preference

canonical_title: Task: write the 'Expected answer' sections in the user's own tone
description: User repeatedly asks to fill the Expected-answer sections of each exercise and to rewrite them in their tone/voice, ending with a request to prompt 'the other claude' to write E_3's expected answer.
why_it_matters: Concrete recurring deliverable task within the assignment; shows a stable voice/tone preference.
confidence_rationale: Explicit user requests across multiple exercises.
inclusion_rationale: Recurring concrete task tied to the deliverable.

canonical_title: Preference: outputs should be written in the user's own tone/voice
description: User asks for content to be rewritten 'using my tone'. The same voice-preservation preference appears strongly in the film-project conversation, suggesting a stable cross-conversation preference.
why_it_matters: A stable user preference relevant to all writing help.
confidence_rationale: Explicit here and echoed elsewhere, but generalization is an inference.
uncertainties: Whether truly stable or task-local

canonical_title: Recurring use of Claude as a prompt-engineering / study & coursework helper
description: Across Oct–Nov 2024 the user repeatedly returns to one chat to build ChatGPT prompts and get help for distinct school/career needs. It is a recurring helper thread, not a single project.
why_it_matters: Captures the true shape of the conversation (a recurring helper) so the distinct sub-activities are not wrongly merged into one project.
confidence_rationale: The recurring pattern is explicit, but framing it as a single 'responsibility' is a modelling choice; the constituent activities are the real entities.
uncertainties: Whether to model the umbrella at all vs. only the sub-activities
contradictions: Selection treated this as one plausible project; Claude finds 5 distinct activities
inclusion_rationale: Emitted to mark over-merge risk; the sub-activities below are the substantive candidates.

canonical_title: Self-study system for catching up on online lectures (beginner, deep understanding)
description: User builds a two-prompt study system: prompt 1 turns lecture transcripts into beginner-friendly segmented teaching; prompt 2 is a concise concept-explainer for things they don't understand. Motivation evolves from exam-prep to deep career-relevant understanding.
why_it_matters: A real owned learning initiative with a deliverable (the prompts) and an evolving goal.
confidence_rationale: Multiple explicit user requests, an artifact (the prompts), an explicit goal change.
uncertainties: Which course the lectures belong to
inclusion_rationale: Distinct owned initiative; meets several project signals (kept as initiative).

canonical_title: Goal change: from exam preparation to deep, career-relevant understanding
description: User explicitly corrects: 'I am not trying to prepare for the exam... I wanted to understand the topic very well since it is useful for my career.'
why_it_matters: A clear user-authored direction change that reframes the study system's purpose.
confidence_rationale: Direct user statement.

canonical_title: Artifact: the lecture-teaching prompt and the concept-explainer prompt
description: Two reusable ChatGPT prompts generated and refined for the user's study system.
why_it_matters: Concrete reusable outputs the user intends to keep using.
confidence_rationale: The prompts are explicit deliverables the user iterated on.

canonical_title: Operating Systems lab (process management & scheduling, Kali Linux)
description: User asks for a prompt and step-by-step help to work through an OS lab (ps, top, kill, renice, cron scheduling) as a beginner, wanting to finish quickly.
why_it_matters: A distinct owned coursework assignment, separate from the lecture-study system.
confidence_rationale: Explicit user statements identifying OS labs, Kali Linux, and specific tasks.
uncertainties: Course identity
inclusion_rationale: Distinct assignment; under three full project criteria so kept as assignment.

canonical_title: Amazon SDE internship application — resume tailoring
description: Sophomore CS student tailoring their existing resume to an Amazon SDE internship job description, with skills/requirements breakdown and ATS optimization.
why_it_matters: A real owned job-application activity with a deliverable (tailored resume) and context (sophomore CS, no prior experience).
confidence_rationale: Explicit user details: Amazon, SDE, sophomore 2nd semester CS, existing resume.
uncertainties: Internship season not stated

canonical_title: YouTube content-policy reviewer prompt (user is an entertainment/vlog creator)
description: User, a YouTube content creator (entertainment/vlogs), wants a prompt that flags policy/copyright/monetization risks in their videos and suggests fixes.
why_it_matters: Reveals an ongoing context (the user runs a YouTube channel) and a small owned tool-building task.
confidence_rationale: Explicit that the user is a creator, but it is a single prompt-building request, not a sustained project.
uncertainties: Whether the channel is active/monetized
inclusion_rationale: Small owned idea plus important user context.

canonical_title: Linear algebra problem-solving & exam questions (step-mirroring prompt)
description: User builds a prompt to mirror example solution steps onto new linear-algebra problems, then pastes actual quiz/exam questions (vector norms, symmetric/orthogonal matrices, quadratic-form classification, unit-sphere extrema).
why_it_matters: Distinct coursework/exam-prep activity; the actual graded questions indicate real academic stakes.
confidence_rationale: Explicit prompt-building plus multiple real exam-style questions pasted.
uncertainties: Whether assistance on graded questions raises an integrity flag (out of scope)
inclusion_rationale: Distinct exam-preparation activity wrongly merged under one project label.

canonical_title: User context: sophomore Computer Science student, beginner, career-focused
description: User identifies as a 2nd-semester sophomore CS student with no prior internship experience, learning OS and linear algebra, applying to internships, and running a YouTube channel.
why_it_matters: Stable user-profile context spanning multiple activities and conversations.
confidence_rationale: Multiple explicit self-descriptions.
uncertainties: Major/year as of later 2026 conversations may have advanced

canonical_title: Amazon (target employer for SDE internship)
description: Amazon as the target employer of the internship-resume-tailoring activity.
why_it_matters: Context entity tied to a job application.
confidence_rationale: Explicitly named by the user.

canonical_title: User's YouTube channel (entertainment/vlog content)
description: The user operates a YouTube channel producing entertainment and vlog content.
why_it_matters: Ongoing user activity/context distinct from coursework.
confidence_rationale: User states they are a creator but channel status/scale unknown.
uncertainties: Activity/monetization status

canonical_title: Personal applied-AI-engineering learning & career roadmap
description: Over multiple days the user co-builds a personalized, research-grounded roadmap to become job-ready as an applied AI engineer: evaluating courses/certs, running deep research (Track A market demand first, then Track B resources), incorporating multiple rounds of feedback, and producing an interactive HTML roadmap tied to their calendar, May-2027 graduation, and DC location.
why_it_matters: The user's central self-directed career project with goals, constraints, decisions, research, an artifact, and clear ownership; high value for reconstruction.
confidence_rationale: Strong evidence across all dimensions: explicit ownership, iterative actions, decisions, constraints, research, an HTML deliverable, repeated appearance.
uncertainties: Exact set of target roles evolves (AI + SWE + infra/data/fullstack)
inclusion_rationale: Meets all nine strict project criteria; clearest true project in the sample.

canonical_title: Decision: research market demand (Track A) before matching resources (Track B)
description: User agrees to prioritize Track A (what the market demands) over Track B (course/resource selection), and to run the five research questions together as one deep-research prompt.
why_it_matters: A concrete user-endorsed sequencing decision driving the roadmap method.
confidence_rationale: User explicitly agrees ('Yes that does make sense and also I agree phase A is more important').

canonical_title: Direction: skip the 23-hour ExamPro GenAI course; prioritize building projects
description: The assistant strongly recommends skipping the ExamPro course/cert in favor of shorter courses and portfolio projects. This is an assistant recommendation; user endorsement is implicit (continued building the roadmap on this basis) rather than an explicit yes.
why_it_matters: Shapes the roadmap, but must be recorded as an assistant-proposed direction with only implicit user continuation.
confidence_rationale: The recommendation is explicit (assistant); the user never explicitly accepts/rejects skipping ExamPro in the preserved units.
uncertainties: No explicit user acceptance of skipping ExamPro is preserved
inclusion_rationale: Recorded as proposed_decision/assistant origin to avoid attributing a user decision to assistant text.

canonical_title: Constraint: avoid spiraling/rabbit-holes; roadmap must be gap-free and competitive
description: User flags that deep research could spiral and asks to avoid it, while requiring the roadmap to make them knowledgeable and competitive with minimal gaps.
why_it_matters: A guiding constraint on how much research/effort to invest.
confidence_rationale: Explicit user statement.

canonical_title: Research questions: 2026 AI-engineering market demand, interviews, startup vs enterprise
description: A set of five research questions (most-demanded skills, what interviews test, startup vs enterprise split, agentic-AI production adoption, highest-leverage skill/project/credential mix for a May-2027 grad) to be run through deep research.
why_it_matters: Defines the evidence base for the roadmap; a real research agenda the user pursued.
confidence_rationale: Explicit question set co-developed and confirmed run ('I have read the researchs').
uncertainties: Research results not preserved in the bundle
inclusion_rationale: Research-question knowledge with explicit user follow-through.

canonical_title: Artifact: interactive HTML AI-engineering roadmap (iterated on feedback)
description: User requests the roadmap as a frictionless interactive HTML they can start the next day, then iterates it across several rounds of external feedback (DSA split, agentic track, Hello Interview, Chip Huyen book, eval tooling).
why_it_matters: The concrete deliverable of the roadmap project, kept current through feedback.
confidence_rationale: Explicit user requests to make/update the HTML and to incorporate feedback.

canonical_title: Source under evaluation: ExamPro GenAI Essentials course/cert
description: A 1-year-old 23-hour ExamPro course/cert the user was about to take; evaluated for currency and credential weight.
why_it_matters: The trigger source for the whole roadmap evaluation.
confidence_rationale: Explicitly the subject of the user's opening questions.

canonical_title: User context: CS junior/senior, DC-based, graduating ~May 2027, prior CodePath work
description: User is a CS student (junior/senior) in the DC area graduating around May 2027, with prior CodePath modules and self-built projects, targeting AI engineering plus SWE/infra/data/fullstack roles.
why_it_matters: Rich, stable user-profile context; ties this conversation to the same user as the 2024 study-helper conversation.
confidence_rationale: Multiple explicit user statements about graduation date, location, CodePath, and target roles.
uncertainties: Whether 'forget CodePath' request means CodePath should be excluded from stored context

canonical_title: Fleet Command — Unity hex-grid space strategy game (semester/final project)
description: A multi-week Unity game project (hex map, ships/cards, tech tree, save/load, combat→map loop, main menu, starting conditions) built from a professor-provided GDD/SDD, developed via AI tools (Bezi, Unity AI, Claude Code), driven toward a submission deadline with professor coordination over Discord.
why_it_matters: A major, sustained, deadline-bound user project; the single richest work entity in the sample for reconstruction.
confidence_rationale: Overwhelming evidence: weeks of phased work, GDD/SDD inputs, many deliverables, decisions, blockers, an HTML tracker, and a submission deadline.
uncertainties: Course name not stated
Final grade/outcome not preserved
inclusion_rationale: Meets all nine strict project criteria.

canonical_title: Professor-provided GDD and SDD (game/software design docs)
description: Game Design Document and Software Design Document the user received from their professor and converted to .md to feed the AI tools; defines the project's scope.
why_it_matters: Authoritative requirements source for the whole project.
confidence_rationale: User explicitly references the GDD/SDD 'I got from professor' and converting them.
uncertainties: Documents themselves not in bundle (attachments listed empty)
contradictions: Bundle is tagged attachment_heavy yet attachment list is empty

canonical_title: Artifact: interactive HTML phased implementation plan / checklist
description: User asks for an interactive HTML artifact to track and check off phases/steps, updated repeatedly as the project progresses.
why_it_matters: The project's living plan/tracker.
confidence_rationale: Explicit request to 'make this an html ... add stuff and check off stuff' and later update it.

canonical_title: Bezi (primary AI coding assistant inside Unity)
description: 'Bezi' is the AI agent the user primarily drives to plan/edit Unity code and scenes; the user repeatedly relays prompts to it and pastes back results.
why_it_matters: Central tool in the project's workflow; the user is actively building with it (not a mere trace).
confidence_rationale: Named and used dozens of times as the build agent.
inclusion_rationale: Adopted/used build tool — meaningful context entity.

canonical_title: Unity AI (evaluated as an alternative/complement to Bezi)
description: Unity's AI assistant; the user obtained ~1500 points and weighed whether to switch from Bezi to it, asking what Unity AI can do that Bezi cannot.
why_it_matters: A tool the user is actively evaluating for adoption.
confidence_rationale: Explicit evaluation, but adoption outcome is not fully resolved.
uncertainties: Whether the user ultimately switched

canonical_title: Workflow: orchestrate Bezi + Unity AI + Claude Code via ask/plan/agent modes
description: User develops a deliberate workflow of asking one assistant to diagnose, having the planner make a plan, confirming, then executing in agent mode, and cross-checking with Claude Code over a GitHub-connected repo.
why_it_matters: A genuine adopted multi-tool development workflow, repeatedly used and refined.
confidence_rationale: Explicit, repeated user descriptions of the ask/plan/agent and cross-tool process.

canonical_title: Decision: pause new features and make the game actually playable first
description: User insists the 'make it playable' work is a crucial prerequisite (not just a sprint) before touching AI, after realizing rushing caused problems; phases must each be verified before moving on.
why_it_matters: A user-authored direction-setting decision and a lesson learned.
confidence_rationale: Explicit user statements re-prioritizing playability and demanding phase verification.

canonical_title: Blocker: Tech Tree panel/button not toggling; UI rendering issues
description: A multi-hour blocker where the tech-tree panel only appeared via manual inspector toggling (missing EventSystem, RectTransform/canvas issues), eventually resolved.
why_it_matters: Representative of the significant UI-debugging effort and a resolved obstacle.
confidence_rationale: Extended, explicit back-and-forth culminating in 'Nice it is working now'.

canonical_title: Milestone/deadline: project submission (final cleanup ~8 hours out)
description: User notes 'submission is in 8 hours we have to finish up the rest and clean up', and coordinates a meeting with the professor via Discord around submission.
why_it_matters: Defines the project's deadline and current/historical state.
confidence_rationale: Explicit deadline statement and professor-coordination messages.

canonical_title: Professor (course instructor; source of GDD/SDD; Discord contact)
description: The user's professor who provided the GDD/SDD and with whom the user coordinates a meeting time over Discord near submission.
why_it_matters: Key stakeholder/context for the project.
confidence_rationale: Explicitly referenced as professor and message recipient.
uncertainties: Name not given

canonical_title: Film-analysis final project on Tsotsi (paper + slides + presentation)
description: Final course project analyzing the film Tsotsi (post-apartheid South Africa): the user selects the film against explicit criteria, drafts a project plan, writes a paper preserving their own voice, builds and revises a PowerPoint, and prepares a ~10–12 minute presentation narration.
why_it_matters: A real owned multi-deliverable academic project spanning weeks; high reconstruction value.
confidence_rationale: Explicit ownership ('final project I am doing'), multiple deliverables, decisions, feedback rounds.
uncertainties: Course/instructor not named
inclusion_rationale: Strong multi-deliverable assignment; kept as assignment (a graded course deliverable) rather than generic project.

canonical_title: Decision: choose the film Tsotsi for the project
description: After weighing options against three criteria (short/easy to write on, enjoyable/intriguing, plentiful data), the user decides 'let us go with Tsotsi'.
why_it_matters: The founding decision of the project and its selection rationale.
confidence_rationale: Direct user statement choosing Tsotsi plus stated selection criteria.

canonical_title: Artifact: the Tsotsi analysis paper (user-written, lightly edited)
description: The user wrote the paper themselves and asked the assistant only to add/polish in their voice, including one added paragraph; later polished for submission.
why_it_matters: A primary deliverable the user authored and owns.
confidence_rationale: User states 'I just wrote it' and directs voice-preserving edits.

canonical_title: Artifact: the presentation slide deck (iterated on visuals + feedback)
description: A PowerPoint built and repeatedly revised (less black, more 'life', real imagery) after the user judged early versions visually poor, plus incorporation of paper feedback and a presentation narration.
why_it_matters: A second primary deliverable, refined over multiple rounds.
confidence_rationale: Multiple explicit revision requests and feedback.

canonical_title: Preference: preserve the user's own wording/voice 'at all cost' in edits
description: User insists edits keep their structure and wording, adding only in their tone and only where needed ('preserve my voice fully').
why_it_matters: A strong, explicit writing preference that recurs (also seen in the Excel conversation).
confidence_rationale: Emphatic, repeated explicit statements.

canonical_title: Preference: no em-dashes in generated writing
description: User instructs 'no em dashes at all'.
why_it_matters: A small but concrete, explicit formatting preference.
confidence_rationale: Direct explicit instruction.
uncertainties: Whether scoped to this project or global

canonical_title: Possible Accenture affiliation (assistant-inferred, not user-confirmed)
description: The assistant infers the user is an Accenture 'Tech Analyst candidate' with an internship starting 'before July', but the user never confirms this in the preserved units.
why_it_matters: Flags an assistant-origin inference that must not be stored as a confirmed user fact.
confidence_rationale: The employer/role context is entirely assistant-inferred; no user-authored confirmation in the bundle.
uncertainties: Whether the user actually has an Accenture internship/role
contradictions: Assistant asserts a Tech Analyst/internship context the user never states
